impactHero.headline: ARIA: Automated Procurement Risk Assessment.
impactHero.subhead: Machine learning identified risk patterns across 3.1 million contracts — comparing every vendor against ~748 documented corruption cases.
impactHero.step1Body: v0.6.5 logistic model: 9 statistical factors per contract
impactHero.step2Body: P1 Monopoly · P2 Shell Company · P3 Intermediary · P6 Capture
impactHero.step3Body: EFOS SAT · SFP · RUPC · ASF — 5 cross-referenced registries

whyItMatters.body: Traditional audit methods review 0.1% of contracts. ARIA scores 100%. The {{t1Count}} Tier 1 vendors share statistical patterns with Mexico's most serious documented corruption cases.

about.title: About ARIA
about.description: The Automated Risk Investigation Algorithm (ARIA) combines ML risk scores, anomaly detection, pattern classification, and external registry cross-referencing (EFOS, SFP, RUPC) to rank vendors by Investigation Priority Score (IPS). Editorial memos are pre-generated by the analysis team using LLM-assisted synthesis of procurement signals.
about.disclaimer: IPS scores are risk indicators, not corruption verdicts. High scores indicate statistical similarity to documented corruption patterns and warrant further investigation.

primer.text: ARIA is RUBLI's automated risk investigation algorithm. It analyzes 318,441 vendors across 4 priority tiers — start with Tier 1 for the highest-priority cases.

header.title: Risk Assessment Center
header.subtitle: {{count}} vendors under active monitoring. Automated risk assessment system for federal public contracting.
header.t1Require: T1 vendors are prioritized for review

editorial.headline: Risk Analysis Briefing
editorial.subtitle: Automated risk queue with priority-level classification

methodology.title: How does ARIA work?
methodology.body: ARIA combines ML risk score (v6.5), PyOD anomaly detection, and cross-referencing with external registries (SAT EFOS, SFP, RUPC) to prioritize investigations. IPS = Integrated Priority Score.

vendorCard.disclaimer: ⓘ Statistical risk indicator — not a legal determination

hero.headline: {{vendorCount}} vendors monitored across 4 priority tiers
hero.subtitle: ARIA monitors federal procurement vendors using ML risk scoring, anomaly detection, and external registry cross-referencing. Start with Tier 1 — the 320 highest-priority cases for review.

patternDesc.P1: Single vendor dominates a market — >60% of sector spend
patternDesc.P2: Shell company signals: no employees, no history, sudden contracts
patternDesc.P3: Vendor acts as pass-through between government and subcontractors
patternDesc.P4: Multiple vendors coordinate bids — rotation or cover bidding
patternDesc.P5: Contract prices significantly above market or sector median
patternDesc.P6: One institution awards >80% of its budget to one vendor
patternDesc.P7: Vendors share addresses, representatives, or RFC patterns

connectionError.title: Connection Error
connectionError.headline: ARIA is unavailable
connectionError.body: The investigation queue could not be loaded. The backend may be restarting — try refreshing in a moment.

explainer.body: ARIA combines nine analysis modules — v6.5 risk scoring, PyOD anomaly detection, co-bidding network analysis, and cross-referencing with external registries (SAT EFOS, SFP, ASF) — to classify 318K+ vendors into four investigation priority levels. T1 vendors are the 320 most critical cases: all show multiple simultaneous warning signals.
